steps.title: Compare the areas
steps.content:
The larger square has side length $3$ times the smaller square's side length. Areas scale by the square of the side-length factor.
$$3^2=9$$
So the larger square has $9$ parts of area for every $1$ part from the smaller square.

steps.title: Use the flux fraction
steps.content: The larger square is $$\frac{9}{10}$$ of the total area. Since the field is uniform and perpendicular to the surface, the larger square gets $$\frac{9}{10}$$ of the total flux. $$\frac{9}{10}(4{,}960)=4{,}464$$ The answer is 4464.
